definition: QT transmits to financial conditions primarily by returning duration to private portfolios, requiring higher term premium compensation. Both New Keynesian (Vayanos-Vila preferred habitat) and Post-Keynesian (endogenous money) frameworks converge on this mechanism. The defensible range for QT-specific term premium impact is 30-60bp, with the remainder of observed moves attributable to fiscal supply, inflation uncertainty, and foreign demand shifts.
evidence: Total observed term premium move ~100bp includes fiscal supply confounding
Treasury issuance roughly doubled during QT2 making clean attribution impossible
Both NK and PK frameworks converge on the directional mechanism
open_questions: Can event-study methodology around QT pace changes (June 2024 taper) help bound the estimate?
If QT contribution is modest (30-60bp) does this make the balance sheet a weaker tightening tool than commonly assumed?
